name: Restaurant Kitchen — 4 Concurrent Stations
scenario: A full-service restaurant kitchen has four stations running simultaneously: cold prep, hot line, grill, and pastry. Each station is its own thread. Orders are dispatched concurrently. The expeditor (main thread) waits for all stations to complete each order before calling 'ticket up!'
description: Simulate a restaurant kitchen with 4 concurrent cooking stations, each implemented as a named Thread with a Runnable. The main thread dispatches an order, all four stations start simultaneously, and main waits with join() for all to complete before announcing the order is done.

core.description: `CookingStation` implements Runnable with stationName, dishName, prepTimeMs. run() prints 'stationName: starting dishName...', sleeps, then prints 'stationName: dishName ready!'
Four stations per order: ColdPrep, HotLine, Grill, Pastry — each with different prep times.
All four station threads started simultaneously using start(). Main uses join() on all four before printing 'Ticket up! Order complete.'
Process THREE orders sequentially (one order = all 4 stations in parallel). Between orders, main prints 'Order N sent to kitchen...'
Each thread named 'Kitchen-StationName' (e.g., 'Kitchen-Grill'). Verify with Thread.currentThread().getName() in output.
Total time for each order is printed: should be ~max(station times), NOT sum.
InterruptedException handled properly in every thread (restore interrupt flag).
main() declares `throws InterruptedException` (or catches at the top level).

bonus.description: Add a 'Head Chef' daemon thread that prints 'Chef: checking quality...' every 1 second. Start it before processing orders. Verify it stops when main exits.
Track which station finishes last (holds up the order). Print 'Bottleneck: ColdPrep (800ms)' at end of each order.
Process 2 orders concurrently (two sets of 4 stations simultaneously — 8 threads total). Print order-level timing.

data_to_use:
Order 1 (Pasta):
  ColdPrep: Caesar Salad — 300ms
  HotLine:  Carbonara — 800ms
  Grill:    Garlic Bread — 400ms
  Pastry:   Tiramisu — 600ms

Order 2 (Steak dinner):
  ColdPrep: Caprese — 200ms
  HotLine:  Ribeye sauce — 1000ms
  Grill:    Ribeye — 1200ms
  Pastry:   Panna Cotta — 500ms

Order 3 (Quick lunch):
  ColdPrep: Side salad — 150ms
  HotLine:  Tomato soup — 400ms
  Grill:    Panini — 350ms
  Pastry:   Cookie — 200ms
example_output:
=== Order 1: Pasta ===
Order 1 sent to kitchen...
[Kitchen-ColdPrep] Starting: Caesar Salad...
[Kitchen-HotLine] Starting: Carbonara...
[Kitchen-Grill] Starting: Garlic Bread...
[Kitchen-Pastry] Starting: Tiramisu...
[Kitchen-ColdPrep] Caesar Salad ready! (300ms)
[Kitchen-Grill] Garlic Bread ready! (400ms)
[Kitchen-Pastry] Tiramisu ready! (600ms)
[Kitchen-HotLine] Carbonara ready! (800ms)
Ticket up! Order 1 complete in 802ms

=== Order 2: Steak dinner ===
...

criteria: All 4 started before any join (15 pts)
Joins wait for all stations (15 pts)
Order time ~= max station time (15 pts)

ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. Key requirement: start ALL station threads before calling join() on any of them. The dispatchOrder method signature: takes order name and array/varargs of Thread or CookingStation. Timing: record System.currentTimeMillis() before starting, after all joins, print diff.
submission_instructions: Save as RestaurantKitchen.java. Push to GitHub. Comment: 'Concurrent kitchen simulation — 4 threads per order, join() for synchronization'.
tips_for_success: Create all 4 Thread objects, call setName(), then start() all four, then join() all four
Record `long orderStart = System.currentTimeMillis()` before first start()
After all joins: `System.out.printf('Ticket up! %s complete in %dms%n', orderName, elapsed)`
For bonus bottleneck: share a volatile reference to track the last-finishing station
For bonus daemon: setDaemon(true) before start(); infinite loop with sleep(1000)
